minds.title: Data Center Power Continuity
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: The data center shall maintain uninterrupted power supply to all critical infrastructure with 99.999% uptime, tolerating any single point of failure in the power distribution chain.

type_specific_attributes.content: Data center power infrastructure shall provide N+1 redundancy at every level from utility feed through UPS to rack PDU, achieving 99.999% availability measured annually.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: Zero unplanned power outages affecting critical load during 12-month measurement period.

minds.title: Network Service Availability
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: All network services shall remain reachable and responsive with latency below 50ms for 99.99% of requests, with automatic failover for any single network component failure.

type_specific_attributes.content: Network infrastructure shall provide redundant paths with automatic failover completing within 5 seconds, maintaining 99.99% service availability measured monthly.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: Service availability >= 99.99% with failover time <= 5 seconds verified during quarterly disaster recovery tests.

minds.title: Power Supply Failure
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Complete or partial loss of electrical power to critical IT equipment due to failure in the power distribution chain from utility feed through UPS to rack-level PDU.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Deploy dual-path power distribution with automatic transfer switches. Maintain UPS battery replacement schedule and monthly generator load-bank testing.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: No single power component failure shall cause loss of power to any critical rack

minds.title: Cooling System Failure
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Data center ambient temperature rises above safe operating limits due to cooling infrastructure failure, triggering thermal shutdown of servers and storage equipment.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Implement N+1 CRAC/CRAH unit redundancy with hot-standby failover. Deploy per-rack temperature sensors with automated alert escalation at 27C, 30C, and 35C thresholds.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Server inlet temperature shall not exceed 27C under any single cooling unit failure condition

minds.title: Network Outage
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Loss of network connectivity between data center zones or to external networks, causing service disruption for end users and inter-service communication failures.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Deploy redundant core switches in active-active configuration with LACP link aggregation. Maintain diverse fiber paths between buildings.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Network failover shall complete within 5 seconds with zero packet loss on stateful connections

minds.title: DNS Resolution Failure
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Domain name resolution service becomes unavailable or returns incorrect results, preventing clients from reaching services by hostname.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Operate geographically distributed recursive resolvers with Anycast addressing. Implement DNSSEC validation and DNS response monitoring.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: DNS query success rate shall remain above 99.99% with response time below 10ms

minds.title: UPS Battery Degradation
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: UPS battery capacity degrades below the minimum runtime threshold required to bridge the gap between utility power loss and generator start-up.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Battery string capacity falls below 80% of rated Ah within 2 years of installation
type_specific_attributes.effects: Insufficient runtime during utility outage, potential unclean server shutdown before generator assumes load
type_specific_attributes.causes: Elevated ambient temperature accelerating plate sulfation, manufacturing defect in cell group, excessive discharge cycling
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Quarterly battery impedance testing with trend analysis and automatic capacity alarm at 85% threshold

minds.title: Generator Start Failure
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Backup diesel generator fails to start or reach stable operating speed within the required transfer time after utility power loss.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Generator fails to reach rated speed and voltage within 10 seconds of start command
type_specific_attributes.effects: UPS batteries depleted without generator backup, complete power loss to critical load after battery exhaustion
type_specific_attributes.causes: Starter motor failure, fuel contamination with water or microbial growth, battery charger malfunction, coolant heater failure in cold weather
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Weekly no-load start test with monthly load-bank test, continuous fuel quality monitoring

minds.title: HVAC Compressor Failure
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: CRAC/CRAH unit compressor fails, reducing cooling capacity below the threshold needed to maintain safe server inlet temperatures.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Compressor motor seizes or refrigerant charge drops below minimum operating pressure
type_specific_attributes.effects: Gradual temperature rise in affected cooling zone, thermal throttling of servers within 15 minutes, thermal shutdown within 30 minutes
type_specific_attributes.causes: Compressor bearing wear, refrigerant leak at brazed joint, electrical contactor failure, condenser coil fouling reducing heat rejection
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Continuous compressor current monitoring with vibration analysis, refrigerant pressure sensors with low-charge alarm

minds.title: Coolant Leak in Chilled Water Loop
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Chilled water piping develops a leak, reducing coolant flow to CRAC/CRAH units and potentially causing water damage to IT equipment below.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Chilled water pipe joint or valve develops leak exceeding 1 liter per hour
type_specific_attributes.effects: Reduced cooling capacity in affected zone, water pooling under raised floor, potential electrical short in equipment below leak point
type_specific_attributes.causes: Pipe joint corrosion, thermal expansion stress on fittings, valve packing wear, water hammer from pump cycling
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Under-floor water leak detection cable with zone identification, chilled water flow rate differential monitoring

minds.title: Switch Port Failure
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Network switch port or line card fails, disrupting connectivity for all devices connected through that port or card.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Switch port enters error-disabled state or line card becomes unresponsive
type_specific_attributes.effects: Loss of connectivity for connected servers or downstream switches, traffic rerouting through redundant path with potential congestion
type_specific_attributes.causes: Transceiver module failure, ASIC defect, firmware bug triggering port flap, electrostatic discharge damage
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: SNMP trap monitoring for port state changes, interface error counter threshold alerts, synthetic traffic probes

minds.title: Fiber Optic Cable Damage
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Fiber optic cable sustains physical damage causing signal attenuation or complete link loss between network devices.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Fiber link optical power drops below receiver sensitivity threshold or link goes down completely
type_specific_attributes.effects: Loss of inter-switch trunk link, traffic failover to alternate path, potential bandwidth reduction during failover
type_specific_attributes.causes: Accidental cable pull during maintenance, bend radius violation in cable tray, rodent damage in conduit, connector contamination
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Optical power monitoring with OTDR baseline comparison, link-down SNMP traps with automated ticket creation

minds.title: DNS Cache Poisoning
minds.creator: fmea-general@example.com
minds.description: Malicious or corrupted DNS records are injected into the resolver cache, causing clients to be directed to incorrect IP addresses.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Resolver cache contains forged A/AAAA records for critical service domains
type_specific_attributes.effects: Clients directed to wrong endpoints, service unavailability or redirection to malicious hosts, data exfiltration risk
type_specific_attributes.causes: Spoofed DNS response accepted due to weak transaction ID randomization, compromised upstream resolver, BGP hijack of authoritative nameserver prefix
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: DNSSEC validation on all responses, DNS response consistency checking across multiple resolvers, anomaly detection on query patterns
